---
title: "12.3 Performance and scalability"
---

## Performance factors

Database performance depends on:
- Hardware: CPU speed, memory size, disk speed, network bandwidth.
- Software: DBMS configuration, query optimization, indexing.
- Workload: Read vs write ratio, query complexity, concurrency level.
- Design: Schema design, normalization, indexing strategy.

## Architecture options

**Centralized architecture.** A single server handles all requests. Simple
but limited scalability.

**Client-server architecture.** Clients handle presentation, server handles
data. The most common architecture for database applications.

**Parallel database systems.** Multiple processors and disks work together
on a single database. Types:

- **Shared memory.** All processors share the same memory and disks.
- **Shared disk.** Each processor has its own memory but shares disks.
- **Shared nothing.** Each processor has its own memory and disks.

![Parallel architectures](images/L12.3_p14.png)

## Scalability strategies

- **Vertical scaling.** Add more resources (CPU, RAM, disk) to existing
  servers.
- **Horizontal scaling.** Add more servers and distribute the data.
- **Replication.** Keep copies of data on multiple servers.
- **Sharding.** Partition data across multiple database instances.
